title: The Valiant Little Tailor
lines: A poor tailor killed seven flies with one blow and made a belt proclaiming 'Seven at a Blow.'
Proud of his deed, he set out to seek his fortune.
He met a giant who challenged him to prove his strength.
The tailor convinced the giant that he had slain seven at once, implying men rather than flies.
The giant was impressed and challenged him to further tests.
Through cleverness and wit, the tailor outwitted the giant at every turn.
He trapped the giant under a tree and defeated him.
The king, hearing of his bravery, offered him his daughter's hand and half his kingdom.
The tailor married the princess and lived happily, using his wits rather than strength to overcome all his challenges.
